California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
March 
■9′  ■990
Barbara 
」. Cu■Ver
City C■erk
City of Pa■oS Verdes Estates
P.0. Box 
■086
Pa■os Verdes Estates′
 CA  90274-0283
Re:
Your Request for Adv■
ce
Our File No. A-90-■
40
Dear us. culver:
You have requested advice regarding your duties as city clerk
with respect to ahe application of the nass mailing provisions of
the Polilica1 Reform Act (the trActn).r This letter confirns
telephone advice provided to you on llarch 7, l99o-
This letter presents an issue which we have not previously
considered. we have provided advice which we bel'j-eve accurately
interprets a iecently adopted regTulation. We recognize that this
is conservative advice on a difficult 
question, therefore, we
intend to provide thi.s Letter to the Commission for review at a
future neeting.
OUESTIONS
1. Uay the city newsletter contain a list of candidates for
electj.on in the city general- etection where one of the candj-dates
is the current mayor of the citY?
2. May the city newsletter reprint a page from the sample
baLlot which tists the candidates?
1' 
Governnent code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
Conmission
regulations appear at.2 California Code of ReguLations section
18ooo, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 
2
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J Street,Suite 800 ● P.0.Box 807 0 Sacramento CA 95804´0807 ● (916)322‐ 5660


our File No. A-90-14 0
Page 2
CONCLUSTON
1. If the newsletter is published conoletelv independentlv
of the mayor, city council, and other elected officers affiliated
!,/ith the city, it roay contain the list of candidates, including
the mayor's name. The mayor's name must not be singled out for
attention by type size, t)peface or type co1or.
2. A page from the sample ballot, shosring the candidates'
names, could not be reprinted in the newsletter.
FACTS
According to the facts which you have provided, the City of
Palos verdes Estates is having its general election in April 1990.
You are the appointed city clerk and write the city ne$rsletter,
which is published quarterly to inform city residents regarding
city issues and events. The edition which precedes the general
eleltion by one week reminds the residents to vote, Iists polling
places, election time, and items on the ballot, such as measures
ind candidates. This year, one of the candidates on the ba11ot is
the current mayor of the city. 
You wish to know if the nayor's
name may be inlluded in the list of candidates without violating
the nasl mailing provision of the Act. 
You have suggested either
a listinq of the candidates be printed or that a page from the
sample billot which lists the candidates be reprinted.
ANALY S I S
Section 89001 of the Act provides that rrno net sletter or
other nass uailing shall, be sent at public expense.rr A nass trail-
ing is defined as two hundred or more substantialty.similar pieces
of mai1. (Section 82041.5.) Regulation I89o12 (copy enctosed)
clarifies that the purpose of section 89001 is to prevent elected
officers from using public funds to send newsLetters and other
rnass mailings at public expense. It Provides that a rnass nailing
may not be sent at public exPense if it contains the narne, office,
photograph or any other reference to an elected officer affiliated
with the agency producing or sending the rnailing and is prepared
or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with
the elected officer. 
(Regrulation 18901(a).)
If the elected officers affiliated with the agency are not
involved in the production or sending of the nailing, so that the
2 A r"vi=ed version of Regulation 18901 $/as adopted by the Con-
roission on December L3, 1989. A statutorily required public com-
nent period expired January 17, 1990. Although the revised ver-
sion of the reg'ulation is stiIl 
in the rulenaking process, it
represents the policy of the Cornrnission. Therefore, we are advis-
ing agencies that they nay rely on it. 
The advice contained in
this letter is based upon the revised version of Regulation 18901.


Our Fi■e No. A-90-■40
Page 3
elected officers′  the items sent may
lude the names of the
elected officers in a manner which does not lrfeaturett them.
(Regulation 18901(a) (2) (A).) In this situation, names of or
references to the elected officers could be included in the text,
provided that they are not singled out by manner of display of the
name or office in the layout, such as by headlines, captions, type
size, typeface, or type coIor. 
(Regul-ation 18901(c)(2).)
A rnailing is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination or concert with an elected officer if the elected
officers affiliated 
with an agency are in any way involved with
the production or distribution of the itens sent. This means, for
exarnple, that the elected officers affiliated with the agency may
not review the urailing or nake sugTgestj.ons for articles to be
included. (Stone Advice Letter, 89-670, copy enclosed.)
An elected officer is affitiated with an agency if he or she
is a nember, officer, or employee of the alrency, or of a subunj.t
thereof, or has supervisory control over the alrency, or appoints
one or more members of the agency. (Regulation 18901(c)(1).) The
rnayor and the city council and any other elected city officials
are included within the definition of elected officers who are
affiliated 
with the city.
Therefore, the nanes of the mayor, the city councilmembers,
or other elected officers affiLiated with the city nay not be
included in the city newsletter if it is produced in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with thern, including such
acts as reviewing the ne\.rsletter or rnaking suggestions for
articles. 
If the newsletter is prepared and sent conpletely
independently of the elected officers, it nay include their narnes
or offices in the text, provided they are not rrfeatured.'r
with respect to your situation, if the pre-electj.on edition
of the newsletter is published comDletelv independentlv of the
mayor, the city council, and any other elected officers affiliated
with the city, the newsletter rnay contain the list of candidates,
including the mayor's name. The mayor's nane must not be singled
out for attention by type size, typeface or type color.
You have also suggested the possibility of including in the
newsletter a reprint of the candidate page fron the sanple ba11ot.
I{hen a jurisdiction urails sanpLe ballots to registered voters as
part of the election process, the nailing does not violate the
nass nailing restrictions, 
because it is nailed pursuant to an
exception. That exception penuits an elected officer's 
name to be
included in an item sent, if the iten is sent as reguired by law
and the use of the elected officer,s name is necessary in the
nailing. 
(Regulation 18901 (f)(6).) 
However, reprinting all or a
portion of the sample baIIot in the city,s nerrsletter is not
required by law. Therefore, the exception for legaI1y-required
rnailings does not apply.


Our Flle No. A-90-■40
,        Page 4
Again, we recognize that this is a conservative
interpretation of Regulation 18901, and we intend to provide this
letter to the Conmission for review at a future neeting.
If you have any questions regarding the above, you may
contact ne at (916) 322-5907.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
多′形包
By: uargaret w. Ellison
Counsel, Legal Division

Enc Iosures
